reasoning: A human vaccine for Hendra virus currently does not exist and is only in Phase I clinical trials. An equine vaccine (Equivac HeV) has been available since 2012 but has not been listed on the specific Wikipedia page for the past 13 years. Because Hendra virus infection is extremely rare in humans (fewer than 10 cases ever), running Phase II/III trials is practically impossible, meaning any human vaccine approval would require alternative, typically slow regulatory pathways. It is therefore highly unlikely a human vaccine will be developed and added to the 'List of infectious diseases' Wikipedia page in the next 6 months. The only pathway to a 'Yes' resolution would be an editor adding the existing equine vaccine or an experimental human vaccine to the list, which has a very low base rate of occurring randomly. I assign extremely low probabilities for all time horizons, strictly adhering to the lower bounds allowed.
